title: Rich Silky Syrian Rice Pilaf
description: A rich and silky Syrian dish featuring vanilla pod, perfect for midnight cravings, this low-carb friendly pilaf is made with fry-grind-rice-khao-khua.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cinnamon, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the uncooked rice and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently, until the rice is coated with the spice mixture.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Fluff the rice with a fork and stir in the split vanilla pod.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the vanilla flavor is absorbed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
